repo.description: Vim-fork focused on extensibility and usability
repo.name: neovim

body:
Introduce _provider_foreach to iterate over all matching provider capabilities for a given LSP method, handling both static and dynamic registrations. Update diagnostic logic and tests to use the new iteration approach, simplifying capability access and improving consistency across features.

Happy to split the first commit from other others. but I thought it was clearer to see the thought process of the different commits

Follow up to #37221

comment_data.body:
should we optional return this if `fn=nil` ? that allows callers to opt-in to get an iterable.

alternatively, instead of `_provider_foreach`, perhaps it should be `_provider_iter` and return a [iterable](https://neovim.io/doc/user/lua/#iterable). So callers can choose what they want to do:

```
vim.iter(_provider_foreach(...)):map(...)
```
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -1230,38 +1230,49 @@ function Client:supports_method(method, bufnr)
   return required_capability == nil
 end
 
---- Retrieves all capability values for a given LSP method, handling both static and dynamic registrations.
---- This function abstracts over differences between capabilities declared in `server_capabilities`
---- and those registered dynamically at runtime, returning all matching capability values.
---- It also handles cases where the registration method differs from the calling method by abstracting to the Provider.
---- For example, `workspace/diagnostic` uses capabilities registered under `textDocument/diagnostic`.
---- This is useful for features like diagnostics and formatting, where servers may register multiple providers
---- with different options (such as specific filetypes or document selectors).
---- @param method vim.lsp.protocol.Method.ClientToServer | vim.lsp.protocol.Method.Registration LSP method name
---- @param ... any Additional keys to index into the capability
---- @return lsp.LSPAny[] # The capability value if it exists, empty table if not found
-function Client:_provider_value_get(method, ...)
-  local matched_regs = {} --- @type any[]
+--- Executes callback fn for all registrations for a given LSP method.
+---
+--- This handles both static capabilities (declared in server_capabilities during
+--- initialization) and dynamic registrations (registered at runtime via
+--- `client/registerCapability`).
+---
+--- Some methods may have multiple registrations (e.g., different documentSelectors
+--- or configurations). The callback is invoked once for each registration.
+---
+--- Example: Getting diagnostic identifiers from all registrations
+---     client:_provider_foreach('textDocument/diagnostic', function(cap)
+---       print(cap.identifier)  -- "static-id", "dynamic-id-1", "dynamic-id-2"
+---     end)
+---
+--- Note: Some capabilities alias to different providers. For example,
+--- `workspace/diagnostic` uses the same `diagnosticProvider` as `textDocument/diagnostic`.
+---
+---@param method vim.lsp.protocol.Method.ClientToServer | vim.lsp.protocol.Method.Registration LSP method name
+---@param fn fun(capability_value: lsp.LSPAny) Callback invoked for each matching capability
+function Client:_provider_foreach(method, fn)
   local provider = self:_registration_provider(method)
+  local required_capability = lsp.protocol._request_name_to_server_capability[method]
   local dynamic_regs = self:_get_registrations(provider)
   if not provider then
-    return matched_regs
+    return
   elseif not dynamic_regs then
     -- First check static capabilities
     local static_reg = vim.tbl_get(self.server_capabilities, provider)
     if static_reg then
-      matched_regs[1] = vim.tbl_get(static_reg, ...) or vim.NIL
+      if vim.tbl_get(static_reg, unpack(required_capability, 2)) then
+        fn(static_reg)
+      end
     end
   else
-    local required_capability = lsp.protocol._request_name_to_server_capability[method]
     for _, reg in ipairs(dynamic_regs) do
       if vim.tbl_get(reg, 'registerOptions', unpack(required_capability, 2)) then
-        matched_regs[#matched_regs + 1] = vim.tbl_get(reg, 'registerOptions', ...) or vim.NIL
+        local cap = vim.tbl_get(reg, 'registerOptions')
+        if cap then
+          fn(cap)
+        end
       end
     end
   end
-
-  return matched_regs

comment_data.body:
I haven't found anywhere where I think we want anything other than a callback/`map`.

The only place I can think of would be potentially in `triggerCharacters` for completion. An LSP could theoretically register multiple times with different triggers.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -1230,38 +1230,49 @@ function Client:supports_method(method, bufnr)
   return required_capability == nil
 end
 
---- Retrieves all capability values for a given LSP method, handling both static and dynamic registrations.
---- This function abstracts over differences between capabilities declared in `server_capabilities`
---- and those registered dynamically at runtime, returning all matching capability values.
---- It also handles cases where the registration method differs from the calling method by abstracting to the Provider.
---- For example, `workspace/diagnostic` uses capabilities registered under `textDocument/diagnostic`.
---- This is useful for features like diagnostics and formatting, where servers may register multiple providers
---- with different options (such as specific filetypes or document selectors).
---- @param method vim.lsp.protocol.Method.ClientToServer | vim.lsp.protocol.Method.Registration LSP method name
---- @param ... any Additional keys to index into the capability
---- @return lsp.LSPAny[] # The capability value if it exists, empty table if not found
-function Client:_provider_value_get(method, ...)
-  local matched_regs = {} --- @type any[]
+--- Executes callback fn for all registrations for a given LSP method.
+---
+--- This handles both static capabilities (declared in server_capabilities during
+--- initialization) and dynamic registrations (registered at runtime via
+--- `client/registerCapability`).
+---
+--- Some methods may have multiple registrations (e.g., different documentSelectors
+--- or configurations). The callback is invoked once for each registration.
+---
+--- Example: Getting diagnostic identifiers from all registrations
+---     client:_provider_foreach('textDocument/diagnostic', function(cap)
+---       print(cap.identifier)  -- "static-id", "dynamic-id-1", "dynamic-id-2"
+---     end)
+---
+--- Note: Some capabilities alias to different providers. For example,
+--- `workspace/diagnostic` uses the same `diagnosticProvider` as `textDocument/diagnostic`.
+---
+---@param method vim.lsp.protocol.Method.ClientToServer | vim.lsp.protocol.Method.Registration LSP method name
+---@param fn fun(capability_value: lsp.LSPAny) Callback invoked for each matching capability
+function Client:_provider_foreach(method, fn)
   local provider = self:_registration_provider(method)
+  local required_capability = lsp.protocol._request_name_to_server_capability[method]
   local dynamic_regs = self:_get_registrations(provider)
   if not provider then
-    return matched_regs
+    return
   elseif not dynamic_regs then
     -- First check static capabilities
     local static_reg = vim.tbl_get(self.server_capabilities, provider)
     if static_reg then
-      matched_regs[1] = vim.tbl_get(static_reg, ...) or vim.NIL
+      if vim.tbl_get(static_reg, unpack(required_capability, 2)) then
+        fn(static_reg)
+      end
     end
   else
-    local required_capability = lsp.protocol._request_name_to_server_capability[method]
     for _, reg in ipairs(dynamic_regs) do
       if vim.tbl_get(reg, 'registerOptions', unpack(required_capability, 2)) then
-        matched_regs[#matched_regs + 1] = vim.tbl_get(reg, 'registerOptions', ...) or vim.NIL
+        local cap = vim.tbl_get(reg, 'registerOptions')
+        if cap then
+          fn(cap)
+        end
       end
     end
   end
-
-  return matched_regs

comment_data.body: Since this is internal it's not a blocker, but I do think as a reflex we should try to return iterables.

comment_regular_data.body:
@tris203, can you do a rebase? Currently, the pull diagnostics for the different identifiers are not working as intended because they are overwriting each other. This should not be a problem in master because `handle_diagnostics` uses namespaces per identifier, so that should fix the issue.

I actually think there might be a bug in master at the moment because workspace diagnostics use namespaces but text document diagnostics do not. As a result, the workspace diagnostics are not getting overwritten by the text document diagnostics. This PR should fix that issue.

Also, another question: should previous resultIds be per diagnostic identifier? When looking at the resultIds being sent from the server (I'm testing with Roslyn), I would assume so. For example, a resultId might be `PublicDocumentPullDiagnosticsHandler(category: DocumentAnalyzerSemantic):3`, where DocumentAnalyzerSemantic is an identifier we sent.

I'm not sure @MariaSolOs @mfussenegger, do either of you maybe know?

> Also, another question: should previous resultIds be per diagnostic identifier? When looking at the resultIds being sent from the server (I'm testing with Roslyn), I would assume so. For example, a resultId might be `PublicDocumentPullDiagnosticsHandler(category: DocumentAnalyzerSemantic):3`, where DocumentAnalyzerSemantic is an identifier we sent.
> 

Yes, from reading the spec. I think we do. The latest commit addresses that

I’ve been testing this branch and think there might be an issue, it looks like I’m getting duplicate diagnostics.

The same diagnostic appears in both a namespace that uses an identifier and one that doesn’t. For example, a diagnostic shows up in both `nvim.lsp.roslyn.2.DocumentCompilerSemantic` and `nvim.lsp.roslyn.2.nil` namespaces
@ZieMcd thank you for testing! So if that doesn't happen on master, that sounds like a blocker before we merge this.

> @tris203 , are you able to replicate?

I have been travelling the last few days. Will take a look at the weekend

ok apologies, I'm pretty sure this is actually bug in nvim-lspconfig for roslyn_ls. It makes a call for text documents it's self also see [here](https://github.com/neovim/nvim-lspconfig/blob/702f69fb167e9119f14adc4dfd4fcadf4d1b07a0/lsp/roslyn_ls.lua#L291).

> ok apologies, I'm pretty sure this is actually bug in nvim-lspconfig for roslyn_ls. It makes a call for text documents it's self also see [here](https://github.com/neovim/nvim-lspconfig/blob/702f69fb167e9119f14adc4dfd4fcadf4d1b07a0/lsp/roslyn_ls.lua#L291).

I concur.

There is some deduplication in the vscode implementation:
https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/3b60cf4f6789b8d3f0df30ae53ed443f0bec9762/src/vs/workbench/contrib/markers/browser/markersModel.ts#L208

But the spec doesn't call for it.

The `nvim-lspconfig` implementation is based on `roslyn.nvim`. And it appears this is some left over workarounds from when the support for dynamically registered diags wasn't there (which this PR aims to fix)

I have a PR to fix it in `roslyn.nvim`(https://github.com/seblyng/roslyn.nvim/pull/330) and then it can go into `lspconfig` this resolves the duplication for me. But would appricate your testing @ZieMcd 
Its very helpful, thank you
Nice, been working well my end since removing that extra request👌

repo.description: Vim-fork focused on extensibility and usability
repo.name: neovim

labels.description: changes that are not features or bugfixes
labels.name: refactor

title: refactor(lsp): replace _provider_value_get with _provider_foreach
